title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, Preconditions, and Status · Compiler Case: Safe Idempotent Cacheable
summary: A task API uses POST for every action, changes state on GET, returns 200 for rejected work, and retries duplicate writes without resource or precondition semantics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts safe idempotent cacheable increment in resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method properties independently and preserve them through application effects.
Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Safe Idempotent Cacheable · Theory Safe Idempotent Cacheable
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1235 in a canary revision draining long-lived response streams. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method properties independently and preserve them through application effects.
